questions.question_text: You have spotted young children crossing the roadway and have reduced your speed to 30 km/h. How will you continue?

answers.1: I give a signal with the horn or a flashing light signal.
answers.2: I continue at an unchanged speed of approximately 30 km/h.
answers.3: I am particularly mindful of the reduced traction.

questions.question_text: You are driving at approximately 50 km/h. How will you continue?

answers.1: I continue at an unchanged speed of approximately 50 km/h.
answers.2: I move my foot over to the brake pedal.
answers.3: I stop before the crosswalk.

questions.question_text: You are about to catch up with the slowly moving motorcyclist, who is travelling at approximately 40 km/h. There are no road users behind you. How will you continue?

answers.1: I reduce my speed.
answers.2: I move into the traffic lane to the left.
answers.3: I remain in the right traffic lane but pull all the way over towards the lane line.

questions.question_text: You intend to turn right at this intersection. There are no road users behind you. How will you continue?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the pedestrian.
answers.2: I warn the pedestrian with the horn.
answers.3: I turn right immediately.
answers.4: I hold back for the pedestrian.

questions.question_text: You are driving at approximately 80 km/h. How will you continue?

answers.1: I continue at an unchanged speed of 80 km/h.
answers.2: I am particularly mindful of the shape of the road ahead.
answers.3: I reduce my speed.
answers.4: I pull over as far as possible to the right of the traffic lane.

questions.question_text: You are driving at approximately 50 km/h and are required to continue straight ahead. How will you continue?

answers.1: I continue across the railway track at an unchanged speed of approximately 50 km/h.
answers.2: I reduce my speed to the minimum before I drive carefully across the railway track.
answers.3: I am particularly mindful of the bicycling and walking children.

questions.question_text: You are driving slowly because you are looking for a particular road. How do you proceed now?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the oncoming traffic.
answers.2: I increase my speed.
answers.3: I wait for the time being before driving past the stationary passenger car.

questions.question_text: What must you be particularly mindful of in this situation?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the crawler lane further ahead.
answers.2: I am particularly mindful that traffic continuing straight ahead must use the right-hand lane further ahead.
answers.3: I am particularly mindful of the rule for changing traffic lanes further ahead.
answers.4: I am particularly mindful of the merge rule further ahead.

questions.question_text: You have taken the wrong road and want to turn around as soon as possible. How do you proceed?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the traffic lights.
answers.2: I drive up to the opening in the central reservation and hold back for any oncoming drivers.
answers.3: When the lane is clear, I turn at the traffic light-controlled intersection.
answers.4: I continue further ahead on the road and find another place to turn.

questions.question_text: What must you be particularly mindful of in this situation?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the shape of the road.
answers.2: I am particularly mindful of the condition of the traffic lane.
answers.3: I am particularly mindful of the crosswalk.

questions.question_text: The oncoming truck has just appeared. You are driving at 40 to 50 km/h. How will you continue?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the oncoming truck.
answers.2: I pull further over to the right of the traffic lane.
answers.3: I continue at an unchanged or only slightly reduced speed.
answers.4: I brake and hold back for the oncoming driver.

questions.question_text: You are driving at approximately 40 km/h and need to pass the stationary passenger car on your right. There are no road users behind you. How will you continue?

answers.1: I pull over to the left side of the traffic lane.
answers.2: I increase my speed to 50 km/h.
answers.3: I drive past the stationary vehicle immediately.
answers.4: I reduce my speed and hold back for the oncoming driver.

questions.question_text: You are driving at approximately 70 km/h, the same speed as the vehicle in front. How will you continue?

answers.1: I continue at an unchanged speed of approximately 70 km/h.
answers.2: I apply my service brake.
answers.3: I am particularly mindful of the traffic further ahead.
answers.4: I am particularly mindful of the condition of the traffic lane.

questions.question_text: You are about to catch up with the red passenger car, which is slowing down. You are required to continue straight ahead at the intersection. How will you continue?

answers.1: I remain in my own traffic lane.
answers.2: I begin to overtake.
answers.3: I am particularly mindful of the grey passenger car that has stopped out in the intersection.
answers.4: I brake and remain behind the red passenger car until I have crossed to the other side of the intersection.

questions.question_text: You are required to continue straight ahead and have just caught up with the braking motorcyclist. There are no road users behind you. How will you continue?

answers.1: I give a flashing light signal with my high-beam headlights.
answers.2: I overtake the motorcyclist immediately.
answers.3: I apply my service brake.

questions.question_text: You have caught up with the slowly moving driver in front. How will you continue?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the road markings on the traffic lane.
answers.2: I cross the centre lines and overtake at the same speed.
answers.3: I overtake at the same speed without crossing the centre lines.
answers.4: I reduce my speed and wait for a while before overtaking the passenger car.

questions.question_text: What must you be particularly mindful of in this situation?

answers.1: I have a duty to give way to all traffic at the intersection.
answers.2: At the intersection, I have a duty to give way only to traffic from the right.
answers.3: I hold back for traffic from both sides.
answers.4: I hold back only for traffic from the right.

questions.question_text: What must you be particularly mindful of in this situation?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful that the expressway is starting.
answers.2: I am particularly mindful that the motorway is starting.
answers.3: I am particularly mindful of the shape of the road.
answers.4: I am particularly mindful that a maximum speed limit of 130 km/h begins here.

questions.question_text: You are driving at approximately 60 km/h and wish to enter the motorway. There are no road users behind you on the motorway itself. How will you continue?

answers.1: I continue at an unchanged or almost unchanged speed of approximately 60 km/h.
answers.2: I increase my speed.
answers.3: I proceed in accordance with the merge rule on the motorway.
answers.4: I merge into the motorway after the grey passenger car.

questions.question_text: You have stepped out of the black passenger car. According to the legal provisions, which of the following applies here?

answers.1: I am permitted to stop the passenger car here for 2 to 3 minutes.
answers.2: I am permitted to park the passenger car here.
answers.3: Stopping and parking are both prohibited here.

questions.question_text: Under what conditions are you permitted to use fog lights on your passenger car?

answers.1: Only in fog and heavy precipitation, and only if they do not disturb other road users.
answers.2: In all weather conditions in order to increase visibility.
answers.3: Only when driving at night, regardless of the weather.

questions.question_text: You are driving on a road with one-way traffic and intend to turn left at the intersection. There are no road users behind you. How will you continue?

answers.1: I continue in my own traffic lane all the way up to the intersection.
answers.2: I move over to the traffic lane to the left of me.
answers.3: I drive all the way up to the intersection immediately.

questions.question_text: You are driving at 40 km/h and are continuing straight ahead through the intersection. How will you continue?

answers.1: I continue across the intersection at an unchanged speed of approximately 40 km/h.
answers.2: I reduce my speed.
answers.3: I move my foot over to the brake pedal.
answers.4: I look to see what is happening on both sides before I drive into the intersection.

questions.question_text: You have stepped out of the red passenger car. According to the legal provisions, which of the following applies here?

answers.1: I am permitted to stop the passenger car here for 2 to 3 minutes.
answers.2: I am permitted to park the passenger car here.
answers.3: Stopping is prohibited here.

questions.question_text: You have stepped out of the red passenger car. According to the legal provisions, which of the following applies here?

answers.1: I am permitted to stop the passenger car here for up to 3 minutes.
answers.2: I am permitted to park the passenger car here.
answers.3: Stopping and parking are both prohibited here.
